---
title: "Global Overview"
root: ".."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
This page provides a visual overview of the global distribution of Alzheimer's disease research initiatives, datasets, and diverse populations.
:::

## Global AD Research Distribution

![World map showing continental distribution of AD research initiatives and datasets]({{< meta root >}}/assets/img/world-map.svg){width=100% fig-alt="World map with continents highlighted showing the scope of this review"}

This map visualizes the geographic scope of this project, highlighting the continents and regions where AD research with diversity considerations is emerging or established.

## Regional Overview

Use the **Interactive Explorer** (in the main navigation) to explore specific:
- **Datasets** by country and modality
- **Geographic focus** by continent
- **Research initiatives** and harmonisation pipelines

### Why Geographic Distribution Matters

- **Data availability** varies dramatically by region
- **Research equity** requires intentional inclusion from underrepresented populations
- **Biological diversity** (ancestry, environmental exposures) enriches our understanding of AD heterogeneity
- **Resource constraints** differ globally, affecting infrastructure for data harmonisation

---

**Next steps:** Explore the [Interactive Explorer](explorer.qmd) or learn more about [datasets](datasets.qmd) and [harmonisation initiatives](atlases-harmonization.qmd).
